county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Winnebago County formed 1840 from Brown County. County seat is Oshkosh on Lake Winnebago. Major Fox River Valley county. One of Wisconsin's greatest lumber cities. University of Wisconsin-Oshkosh. EAA AirVenture. Strong German settlement.
county_metadata.research_tips: Winnebago County Register of Deeds in Oshkosh holds land records from 1840. Major Fox River Valley county on Lake Winnebago. Oshkosh was one of Wisconsin's greatest lumber cities -- lumber baron estates and company records at WHS. The Oshkosh Public Museum and Winnebago County Historical and Archaeological Society have excellent collections. University of Wisconsin-Oshkosh archives. EAA (Experimental Aircraft Association) AirVenture Museum. Very strong German and Yankee settlement. Oshkosh city directories from the 1860s. Paine Art Center and Gardens. FamilySearch has indexed Winnebago County records. BLM GLO patents. Catholic and Lutheran church records essential.
county_metadata.online_land_records_scope: BLM GLO federal land patents for Winnebago County; county deed books at the Register of Deeds office in Oshkosh; WHS has microfilm of historical deed books

source_profiles.source_name: Winnebago County Deed Books -- Register of Deeds
source_profiles.repository: Winnebago County Register of Deeds, Oshkosh
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Original deed books for Winnebago County from 1840; grantor/grantee indexes; mortgages and liens; Register of Deeds office maintains land records. WHS has microfilm of historical deed books
source_profiles.survival_notes: Excellent survival from 1840; no major record losses

source_profiles.source_name: Winnebago County Federal Land Patents -- BLM GLO
source_profiles.repository: Bureau of Land Management, General Land Office Records
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Federal land patents (preemption, homestead, cash entry, military bounty) for Winnebago County; Wisconsin is a public land state and GLO patents document original federal land transfers to private ownership

Wills, administrations, inventories, guardianships, and estate settlements for Winnebago County from 1840; Probate Court has jurisdiction over estate matters; WHS has microfilm of historical probate records

source_profiles.source_name: Winnebago County Marriage Records -- County Clerk / WHS / FamilySearch
source_profiles.repository: Winnebago County Clerk, Oshkosh / Wisconsin Historical Society, Madison
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Marriage records for Winnebago County from 1840; County Clerk issued marriage licenses in Wisconsin; WHS holds some county marriage records on microfilm; FamilySearch has indexed Wisconsin marriages; state vital records registration began 1907 (sporadic earlier compliance)

source_profiles.source_name: Winnebago County Federal and State Census Records
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Federal census 1840-1950 for Winnebago County; Wisconsin state census 1855, 1865, 1875, 1885, 1895, 1905 at WHS (unusually detailed -- include place of birth, occupation, citizenship status); territorial census 1836 and 1842 may include Winnebago County entries

source_profiles.source_name: Winnebago County Lumber Industry Records -- WHS
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Lumber company records for Winnebago County at WHS including employment ledgers, payrolls, company store accounts, logging camp records, and land holdings; essential for tracing workers in the northern Wisconsin timber industry; circuit court records also document labor disputes and land claims

source_profiles.source_name: Winnebago County Cemetery Records -- Find A Grave / WI GenWeb
source_profiles.repository: Find A Grave / Wisconsin GenWeb / Winnebago County Historical Society
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Cemetery transcriptions for Winnebago County including church cemeteries, municipal cemeteries, and pioneer family burial grounds; Find A Grave has growing coverage; Wisconsin GenWeb and local historical societies have published transcriptions; DAR chapters have documented many Wisconsin cemeteries

county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Wood County formed 1856 from Portage County. County seat is Wisconsin Rapids (formerly Grand Rapids/Centralia). Central Wisconsin paper and cranberry industry county. Very strong Polish and German settlement. Marshfield Clinic.
county_metadata.research_tips: Wood County Register of Deeds in Wisconsin Rapids holds land records from 1856. Created from Portage County -- check Portage County for pre-1856 records. Central Wisconsin county with major paper and cranberry industries. Very strong Polish and German immigrant settlement. Wisconsin Rapids (formerly Grand Rapids, Centralia) paper mill records document workers. The South Wood County Historical Corporation in Wisconsin Rapids maintains collections. Marshfield has a separate strong archival tradition (Marshfield Clinic records). WHS has microfilmed Wood County records. Catholic and Lutheran parish records (esp. Polish Catholic) are essential. FamilySearch has some Wood County records. BLM GLO patents.
county_metadata.online_land_records_scope: BLM GLO federal land patents for Wood County; county deed books at the Register of Deeds office in Wisconsin Rapids; WHS has microfilm of historical deed books

source_profiles.survival_notes: Excellent survival from 1856; no major record losses
